---
title: "Day N: Topic"
subtitle: "Morning brief, Weekday Month D"
author: "Dan Negrut"
date: 2026-07-DD
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../theme.scss]
    slide-number: true
    width: 1250
    margin: 0.08
    transition: fade
    footer: "PROCSI C Training  |  Day N"
    embed-resources: true
---

<!--
Reusable template for a morning briefing deck. Copy this file to
days/day-NN-topic.qmd, fill in the placeholders, and delete these comments.
Aim for 5 to 6 slides total (this title slide counts as one). Keep bullets
short so nothing runs off the bottom; tag a fuller slide `## Title {.smaller}`.
The starter line on "How to start" should match the tiered policy: nothing on
the low-floor days, a skeleton/helper/demo on the high-ceiling days.
-->

## Where this fits

- One line on why today matters and what it connects to (the through-line).
- Where it lands in the two-week arc.

## Today's target

- **By 3:30, your team can:** <one concrete, checkable goal>.
- Keep it small and reachable; save the fancy stuff for the stretch goal.

## How to start

- Tools: <what to open or install>.
- Starter: <the day's starter, or "nothing, start from a blank slate">.
- Ask Claude: <one or two prompt ideas to get unstuck>.

## Pointers

- <key command, concept, or link 1>
- <key command, concept, or link 2>
- <key command, concept, or link 3>

## Your end-of-day talk

- ~10 minutes: what you tried, how far you got, what you learned, what stumped you.
- **Close with your team's quote or joke of the day.**
- Stretch goal (optional): <a nice-to-have if you finish early>.
